channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Durban authorities target crime-ridden building in CBD crackdown
item.description: A multi-departmental operation was launched in Durban's Central Business District to address a building identified as a major crime generator. The operation, conducted on Lancers Road near health faci...

item.title: TISM's satirical approach to interviews and their enduring cult status
item.description: The article explores the Australian band TISM's unique approach to interviews, where they blend satire and self-deprecation to engage with their fans. Formed in the 1980s, TISM is known for their prov...

item.title: Unsolved Edmonton Murder of Nurse Nadine Robinson Still Lacks Closure After 20 Years
item.description: Nadine Robinson, a 36-year-old nurse from Edmonton, was found dead in her townhouse in 2006. Her death remains unsolved, with no charges filed against any suspect. Her son, Stephen Robinson, expressed...

item.title: Moshi Region Faces Land Use Conflict Between Livestock Farmers and Agricultural Owners
item.description: Authorities in Moshi have issued a stern warning to livestock keepers in the region, cautioning them against allowing their animals to trespass onto agricultural land as tensions between herders and c...

item.title: FG Denies Defence Minister's Resignation Rumours
item.description: The Federal Government has officially denied reports suggesting that Defence Minister General Christopher Musa (retd.) is planning to resign. A statement released by the Minister of Defence's office c...

item.title: Spanish Soccer Star's Viral Handshake With Trump Sparks Global Reaction Amid FIFA World Cup
item.description: The FIFA World Cup has become a global stage for both sporting excellence and political intrigue. Lamine Yamal, the young Spanish soccer prodigy, recently made headlines not for his on-field performan...

item.title: US Condemns China's Aggressive Actions in South China Sea Clash with Philippine Navy
item.description: The United States has condemned China's 'dangerous and aggressive' actions during a recent incident in the South China Sea, where Chinese Coast Guard personnel allegedly injured a Filipino sailor. The...

item.title: Government Restricts Private Sector from Importing High-Speed Diesel
item.description: The Pakistani government has imposed a complete ban on private oil marketing companies (OMCs) from importing high-speed diesel (HSD) for the fiscal year 2027. Instead, state-owned Pakistan State Oil (...

item.title: British man arrested at Tenerife South Airport over alleged attempted murder following 2025 knife attack in Adeje
item.description: A 46-year-old British man has been arrested by Spain's National Police at Tenerife South Airport after arriving on a flight from Liverpool. He was wanted in connection with an alleged attempted murder...

item.title: CNN Debate Over Trump Border Czar's Controversial Comments on ICE Practices

item.title: Mary Habila, Community Health Worker, Found Dead at Minister's Residence Amid Controversy
item.description: Mary Habila, a young woman found dead at the residence of Nigeria's Minister of Works and former Ebonyi State Governor, David Umahi, was revealed to be a Community Health Extension Worker (CHEW) rathe...

item.title: ActewAGL invites residents to share photos capturing the unique energy of the ACT and south-east NSW
item.description: ActewAGL, the local energy provider for the ACT and south-east NSW, is launching a community photo contest titled 'Capture the Energy' to celebrate the region's unique character. The initiative invite...

item.title: Supreme Court Orders Report on Imran Khan's Jail Meetings
item.description: The Supreme Court of Pakistan has directed the Adiala jail administration to submit a comprehensive report detailing the meetings between incarcerated PTI founder Imran Khan and his family members, la...

item.title: Fark.com Struggles with Ad Revenue and Survival Concerns
item.description: Fark.com, a long-standing community-driven website known for its eclectic mix of user-submitted links, absurd headlines, and internet humor, is facing significant challenges in maintaining ad revenue....

item.title: South Africa's 15-Minute City Trend: Developers Create Self-Sufficient Neighborhoods
item.description: The article explores the growing trend of '15-minute cities' in South Africa, where developers are creating self-sufficient neighborhoods to address urban challenges. As rising fuel costs, traffic con...

item.title: Ombudsman to Investigate Alleged Plunder and Money Laundering Against Former House Speaker Martin Romualdez
item.description: The Office of the Ombudsman announced it will file a preliminary investigation against former House Speaker Martin Romualdez, along with others, over allegations of plunder and violations of the Anti-...

item.title: HBO's Harry Potter Series Faces Challenges with Young Cast Scheduling
item.description: The cinematographer for HBO's upcoming Harry Potter series, Adriano Goldman, has described the production process as 'brutal' due to the strict scheduling constraints imposed on young actors. Filming ...

item.title: CFL Player Dies in Fatal Crash Involving SUV Driver Going the Wrong Way
item.description: A fatal crash involving a Saskatchewan Roughriders player has raised concerns about road safety and substance use. RCMP confirmed that the driver of a silver SUV was traveling the wrong way on a divid...

item.title: Ariel Winter Returns as Voice of Sofia in New Disney Series
item.description: Ariel Winter, the voice behind Disney's 'Sofia the First,' is back for a new spin-off series titled 'Sofia the First: Royal Magic.' The show, premiering on Disney Jr. and Disney+, follows Sofia as she...

item.title: Carnapper Injured in Accident Involving Stolen License Plate
item.description: A carnapper, identified as Christopher Sitson of Barangay San Isidro, Antipolo, Rizal, was involved in a traffic incident on July 21, 2026. According to ABS-CBN News, the individual sustained injuries...
